inputs.userGoal: Create an Azure IoT Hub edge deployment with AI module for anomaly detection, output events to Cosmos DB, and send SMS alerts via Logic Apps

reasoning.whyChosen: Dedicated resource group provides logical isolation and simplified management of all IoT edge components. East US chosen for cost-effectiveness and broad service availability.
reasoning.tradeoffs: East US provides lower latency for East Coast operations but may have higher latency for global access

resources.name: iothub-edge-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: IoT Hub S1 tier provides sufficient message throughput for edge devices, supports device-to-cloud and cloud-to-device messaging. Enables IoT Edge runtime management and module deployment.
reasoning.tradeoffs: S1 provides good balance of cost and features; higher tiers offer more throughput but at increased cost

resources.dependencyReasoning: Must be deployed in the resource group

resources.name: vm-iot-edge-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Standard_B2s VM provides adequate compute for IoT Edge runtime and AI modules with burstable CPU. Ubuntu 20.04 LTS is well-supported for IoT Edge deployments.
reasoning.tradeoffs: B2s is cost-effective for variable workloads but may need scaling for high-throughput AI processing

resources.dependencyReasoning: VM requires virtual network for connectivity and network security group for access control

resources.name: vnet-iot-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Dedicated VNet provides network isolation for IoT edge devices with private IP addressing. Single subnet sufficient for initial deployment.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Simple single-subnet design reduces complexity but may require redesign for complex multi-tier architectures

resources.dependencyReasoning: VNet must be created in the resource group

resources.name: nsg-iot-edge-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Network Security Group provides firewall rules for IoT Edge VM. Allows SSH access for management and HTTPS for IoT Edge agent communication.
reasoning.tradeoffs: NSG provides basic security at lower cost but lacks advanced threat protection of Azure Firewall

resources.dependencyReasoning: NSG must be created in the resource group

resources.name: cog-anomaly-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Cognitive Services Anomaly Detector provides pre-built AI models for time-series anomaly detection. S0 tier offers good balance of cost and performance for production workloads.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Managed service reduces development time but limits customization compared to custom ML models

resources.dependencyReasoning: Cognitive Services account must be created in the resource group

resources.name: cosmos-iot-events-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Cosmos DB provides globally distributed, low-latency storage for IoT telemetry data. Session consistency balances performance and data consistency for IoT scenarios.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Cosmos DB offers excellent scale and performance but at higher cost than traditional SQL databases

resources.dependencyReasoning: Cosmos DB account must be created in the resource group

resources.name: acs-sms-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Azure Communication Services provides SMS capabilities for sending alerts. Global deployment with US data location for compliance.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Native Azure service provides better integration but may have limited global SMS coverage compared to specialized providers

resources.dependencyReasoning: Communication Services must be created in the resource group

resources.name: logic-sms-alerts-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Logic Apps provides serverless workflow orchestration to process anomaly events and trigger SMS alerts. Standard tier offers good performance and reliability.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Logic Apps simplifies workflow design but may have higher cost than custom Functions for simple scenarios

resources.dependencyReasoning: Logic App needs Communication Services for SMS functionality

resources.name: st-iotedge-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Storage account required for IoT Edge modules, container images, and logging. Standard LRS provides cost-effective local redundancy for non-critical data.
reasoning.tradeoffs: LRS offers lowest cost but no geo-redundancy; acceptable for development logs and temporary data

resources.dependencyReasoning: Storage account must be created in the resource group

resources.name: kv-iot-edge-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Key Vault securely stores connection strings, certificates, and secrets for IoT Edge modules and services. RBAC authorization provides fine-grained access control.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Centralized secret management improves security but adds complexity compared to direct configuration

resources.dependencyReasoning: Key Vault must be created in the resource group

overallReasoning.summary: This architecture implements a complete IoT Edge solution with AI-powered anomaly detection. IoT devices connect to IoT Hub, which manages edge devices running anomaly detection modules. Detected anomalies are stored in Cosmos DB and trigger SMS alerts via Logic Apps and Communication Services. The solution provides scalable, secure, and managed services for IoT data processing.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: Uses managed Azure services for faster deployment and reduced maintenance overhead, but at higher cost than custom implementations. Single-region deployment reduces complexity but lacks geo-redundancy for disaster recovery.

plan.validation: All resources are properly sized for a production IoT Edge deployment. Dependencies are correctly ordered, with networking infrastructure created before compute resources. Security is addressed through Key Vault for secrets management and NSGs for network access control. The architecture supports the complete flow from IoT devices through edge processing to alerting.
